Requirement Text: Implementation SHALL comply with all normative references. In case of conflict, referenced standards prevail.
No issuer master key SHALL be stored or accessible inside the kernel.
Kernel SHALL NOT generate ARQC, TC, or AAC; these cryptograms are returned by the card.
CAPKs are public keys requiring integrity and authenticity but not confidentiality.
PIN processing SHALL follow certified integration model (Model A); kernel SHALL NOT access clear PIN.
Kernel SHALL select PSE using exact contact APDU and PPSE using exact contactless APDU.
Kernel SHALL parse valid GPO response formats 77 and 80.
Kernel SHALL extract AIP and AFL or fail with explicit missing mandatory tag.
Kernel SHALL validate SFI and record range before READ RECORD.
Kernel SHALL encode READ RECORD P2 as (SFI << 3) | 0x04.
Kernel SHALL parse record templates into card data without unmasked sensitive logging.
Kernel SHALL reject inconsistent or malformed cardholder PAN data between tags 5A and 57 without partially updating the card data store.
Kernel SHALL use symbolic constants for TVR bits; raw hex SHALL NOT be used directly.
Kernel SHALL clear TVR before each transaction.
Kernel SHALL implement all RFU bits as reserved; SHALL NOT set them.
Kernel SHALL set TSI bits as defined.
Kernel SHALL accept Terminal Capabilities tag 9F33 through a stable ABI and use it in DOL data.
Kernel SHALL accept Terminal Transaction Qualifiers tag 9F66 through a stable ABI and use it in contactless DOL data.
Kernel SHALL decode CID using mask 0xC0 and mapping.
Kernel SHALL parse CVM List, evaluate condition codes, enforce amount limits.
Kernel SHALL set TVR byte 3 bits based on CVM outcome.
Kernel SHALL use the certified CVM code table.
Kernel SHALL accept terminal and PED CVM capabilities through a stable ABI.
Kernel SHALL store CVM Results as tag 9F34 with three EMV result bytes.
Kernel SHALL distinguish offline plaintext offline enciphered and online PIN methods.
Kernel SHALL NOT expose clear PIN values in kernel memory.
Kernel SHALL delegate PIN block construction to the PED or secure PIN module.
Kernel SHALL encode P1 for GENERATE AC using constants 0x00, 0x40, 0x80.
If CDA supported and enabled, kernel SHALL set CDA request bit per scheme profile.
Kernel SHALL treat CDA request encoding as profile-defined and unsupported when absent.
Kernel SHALL fetch IACs from card; missing tags use signed profile fallback values.
Kernel SHALL fetch TACs from signed configuration.
Kernel SHALL evaluate TAA decision steps in exact order; fallback policy explicitly configured.
Kernel SHALL use deterministic profile fallback policy for TAA no-match/default cases.
Kernel SHALL implement state-specific handling for all SW codes in full state machine.
Kernel SHALL NOT treat all non-9000 responses as generic errors.
Kernel SHALL verify CAPK hash before use; if invalid treat as missing.
CAPKs require integrity and authenticity; confidentiality not required.
If issuer public key certificate recovery fails, set appropriate TVR bit and continue to TAA.
If ICC public key certificate recovery fails, set DDA or CDA failure TVR bit.
SDA failure sets TVR_B1_SDA_FAILED.
DDA failure sets TVR_B1_DDA_FAILED.
CDA failure sets TVR_B1_CDA_FAILED; if CDA supported and fails, SHALL NOT fall back to DDA.
Kernel SHALL verify CDA signed dynamic data against the first GENERATE AC cryptogram using profile-defined rules.
Kernel SHALL use INTERNAL AUTHENTICATE only for DDA where the AIP card data and profile require or allow it.
Kernel SHALL verify signed dynamic application data using the recovered ICC public key.
Kernel SHALL reject ODA certification vectors containing placeholders truncated cryptographic material non-hex characters or missing expected outputs.
Kernel SHALL process tag 91 issuer authentication data.
Kernel SHALL set TSI only when issuer authentication is attempted.
Kernel SHALL set TVR issuer authentication failed on card failure.
Kernel SHALL construct CDOL2 from card profile data and host response data.
Kernel SHALL include issuer authentication or authorization response data where required by CDOL2.
Kernel SHALL issue second GENERATE AC where required after online authorization.
Kernel SHALL treat final TC as approval and final AAC as decline.
Kernel SHALL validate issuer script template structure.
Kernel SHALL execute issuer script commands in order.
Kernel SHALL capture issuer script command SW1/SW2.
Kernel SHALL set before-final-GAC script failure TVR bit.
Kernel SHALL set after-final-GAC script failure TVR bit.
Kernel SHALL report issuer script SW1/SW2 results to Level 3 for host reporting.
Kernel SHALL implement C-8 unified kernel for contactless transactions.
Kernel SHALL return outcome parameters via contactless_outcome() callback.
Kernel SHALL NOT treat C-8 as contact kernel replacement.
Kernel SHALL reject example-only profiles from certification or production loading.
Kernel SHALL NOT be re-entrant; caller SHALL serialise calls.
Buffers passed to callbacks are owned by caller; kernel SHALL NOT free them.
Kernel SHALL provide bounded callback timeouts for APDU transport and terminal integration callbacks.
Kernel SHALL return stable error codes retrievable after terminal outcome.
PED API SHALL return status and secure handles only.
Online PIN encrypted blocks SHALL NOT be copied into general kernel memory.
Kernel SHALL enforce formal log policy; production builds SHALL NOT include debug logging that can be enabled without cryptographic authorisation.
Kernel SHALL obtain unpredictable numbers from an approved platform RNG callback.
Kernel SHALL reject all-zero, all-FF, or repeated unpredictable numbers.
Kernel SHALL define every error code in a stable ABI table.
Kernel SHALL fail closed where standards or profile do not explicitly permit continuation.
Kernel SHALL support signed configuration updates.
Kernel SHALL reject rollback or replayed configuration versions.
Kernel SHALL apply configuration updates atomically.
Kernel SHALL retain versioned configuration identity in transaction logs.
Kernel SHALL achieve EMVCo Level 2 certification for each claimed scheme and interface.
Kernel SHOULD pass third-party penetration test on APDU injection and state machine bypass.
SHALL pass automated schema validation before release.
SHALL not contain semantic contradictions such as next_state = SE with an action saying "jump to TAA."
SHALL classify DOLs as DOL values, not constructed TLV templates.
SHALL reject placeholder values, ellipses, non-hex material, dummy AIDs, dummy RIDs, and dummy CAPKs from any production or certification profile.
SHALL contain complete cryptographic vectors. Scenario summaries are not test vectors.
SHALL construct all APDUs according to ISO/IEC 7816-4 and EMV command-specific rules.
SHALL handle APDU response status words by command and state, not by a single global success/failure rule.
SHALL implement at least the following status-word categories where applicable:
SHALL validate abi_version and struct_size before dereferencing optional fields.
SHALL fail initialization if mandatory callbacks are absent.
SHALL define every amount in minor units and SHALL bind currency exponent handling to terminal/acquirer configuration.
SHALL define all bitmaps using symbolic constants and one canonical mapping file. Implementation code SHALL NOT use unexplained raw bitmap literals.
SHALL reject CAPKs whose signed configuration integrity cannot be verified.
SHALL treat expired CAPKs as unavailable unless a lab-approved test profile explicitly requires expiry simulation.
SHALL obtain EMV Level 2 approval for every claimed interface, kernel, and scheme profile.

Requirement Text: SHALL not present illustrative profiles or placeholder vectors as certification evidence.
SHALL verify digital signature before using any configuration data.
SHALL reject configurations with invalid schema, unknown mandatory fields, expired CAPKs, invalid AID encoding, invalid hex strings, non-hex key material, or invalid terminal parameter lengths.
SHALL implement rollback protection using monotonic versioning or secure counters.
SHALL preserve non-type CID bits for trace logging and scheme/profile handling, but SHALL NOT use them to change cryptogram type classification unless the licensed specification/profile requires it.
SHALL use PPSE 2PAY.SYS.DDF01 for contactless Entry Point.
SHALL return structured contactless outcome data rather than only generic approve/decline status.
SHALL evaluate contactless transaction limit, CVM limit, and floor limit using signed scheme/acquirer configuration.
SHALL treat CDCVM as profile-defined. The kernel SHALL NOT assume that any single tag or bit universally proves CDCVM without scheme/profile validation.
SHALL implement relay-resistance APDUs, timing constraints, and result handling only where the licensed profile requires or certifies them.
SHALL NOT treat CDCVM as a universal method code unless the active contactless profile explicitly defines that mapping.
SHALL construct PDOL, CDOL1, CDOL2, DDOL, and TDOL data by resolving each requested tag from the terminal/card/kernel context and appending the exact requested length.
SHALL use zero padding or profile-defined defaults only where permitted by EMV and scheme rules.
SHALL implement the full transition table as a machine-readable artifact with RFC 4180 valid CSV or JSON.
SHALL distinguish fatal protocol errors from risk conditions. A missing mandatory tag may be fatal in one state and TVR-mediated in another only if the licensed standard permits that behavior.
SHALL preserve enough context to replay a transaction deterministically for certification debugging, excluding clear PIN and excluded sensitive data.
SHALL process card removal, APDU timeout, host timeout, and callback failure through explicitly documented transitions.
SHALL construct CDOL1 and CDOL2 data exactly from the active DOL definition.
SHALL encode requested cryptogram type using 0x00, 0x40, or 0x80 request bits, plus only licensed/profile-defined lower-bit flags.
SHALL NOT use 0x80 as a "first GENERATE AC flag."
SHALL parse returned CID by CID & 0xC0 and shall parse AC, ATC, IAD, and any response template data according to EMV/profile rules.
SHALL build CDOL1 from tag 8C where present, or from profile-defined defaults where permitted.
SHALL include the unpredictable number, TVR, transaction amount, date, type, terminal country, CVM results, and other requested data exactly as CDOL1 requires.
SHALL request AAC, TC, or ARQC according to TAA.
SHALL parse response format 1 or 2 as permitted by the active profile.
SHALL if CDA was requested, verify CDA before accepting the returned cryptogram outcome.
SHALL separate contact and contactless kernel selection in configuration.
SHALL NOT treat Book C-8 as a contact kernel unless a specific licensed specification and certification approval explicitly support that use.
SHOULD use Book C-8 for contactless where the product, scheme set, region, terminal class, and certification scope support C-8.
SHALL reject a transaction if no certified kernel/profile mapping exists for the selected AID and interface.
SHALL disable full APDU logging in production unless certified support mode is enabled by policy.
SHALL exclude cardholder data, clear PIN, PIN block, and secret material from crash dumps.
SHALL make debug builds cryptographically and operationally distinguishable from production builds.
SHALL pass online authorization data to Level 3 without performing acquirer/issuer host role.
SHALL receive host response data, including authorization response code, issuer authentication data, and issuer scripts, through the Level 3 callback/API.
SHALL measure ODA RSA/ECC operations separately from TLV parsing and APDU overhead.
SHALL define certification performance targets in the product profile, not in generic prose.
SHALL interpret 63 Cx from VERIFY as PIN verification warning with tries remaining and update TVR/CVM results accordingly.
SHALL reject placeholder strings such as ..., dummy RIDs, dummy AIDs, and non-hex CAPK values in production or certification configuration.
SHALL validate all CAPK modulus/checksum strings as hex and expected length before loading.
SHALL evaluate processing restrictions in the order required by EMV and scheme profile.

SHALL NOT create non-standard TVR bits for currency mismatch or service mismatch. The implementation shall express such conditions using the EMV-defined AUC/service/version/date conditions or profile-defined terminal decision inputs.
SHALL use 2PAY.SYS.DDF01 for contactless PPSE selection.
SHALL support direct AID selection when PSE/PPSE is absent, unsupported, or profile rules require direct selection.
SHALL evaluate denial before online approval requests.
SHALL include IAC values in TAA whenever available or required by the application/profile.
SHALL NOT allow an unconstrained configuration field such as default_cryptogram to override EMV or scheme rules.
SHALL parse primitive and constructed BER-TLV objects according to EMV and ISO/IEC 7816-4 rules.
SHALL parse DOLs as tag-length sequences, not as constructed TLV templates.
SHALL reject malformed tags, unsupported indefinite length encoding, lengths exceeding configured maxima, truncated values, or invalid nested templates.

SHALL maintain a machine-readable TLV catalogue with columns: tag, name, type, length rule, source, interface applicability, scheme applicability, presence rule, sensitive-data classification, and test IDs.
SHALL mark scheme-specific, proprietary, and RFU tags as PROFILE-DEFINED and shall not assign invented semantics.

SHALL admit card-originated AFL record TLVs only from direct primitive Template 70 children and shall reject terminal-owned, kernel-owned, or host-response-owned tags without partially updating the transaction data store.
SHALL implement floor-limit checking per profile and transaction type.
SHALL implement random transaction selection only according to EMV/profile rules. The algorithm and parameters shall be certified as part of the profile.
SHALL maintain offline counters only in non-volatile storage where the active scheme/profile requires terminal-maintained counters.
SHALL set TSI terminal risk management performed when TRM has executed.
SHALL set TSI bits only after the corresponding function has actually been performed.
